rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ENEZ A. BESSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Costs By Joscelyn Jones As Objector
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2} This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Objector's Motion to Strike or Tax Costs is DENIED. The Memorandum of Costs on Appeal was timely filed. Under California Rule of Court 8.278(c)(1), the Memorandum of Costs on Appeal must be filed within 40 days of the issuance of the remitter. Under California Rule of Court 1.10(a), "The time in which any act provided by these rules is to be performed is computed by excluding the first day and including the last, unless the last day is a Saturday, Sunday, or other legal holiday, and then it is also excluded." Here, the remittitur is date-stamped April 1, 2021 by the appellate clerk. (Lvovich Decl., Exh. A.) Excluding the first day (April 1, 2021) and including the 40th day, the deadline for filing the Memorandum of Costs following the remittitur was May 12, 2021. The verified Memorandum of Costs on Appeal was filed on May 11, 2021. The Memorandum of Costs on Appeal must be verified and submitted on the mandatory Judicial Council form, which contains the requisite verification language. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 8.278(c)(1).) The filing party is not required to include any supporting documentation with the Memorandum; the verified document establishes a prima facie showing that the costs listed in the Memorandum were necessarily incurred. The party moving to strike the Memorandum of Costs or to tax requested costs must establish that a disputed item is not recoverable or was not actually incurred. (Alan S. v. Sup.Ct. (Mary T.) (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 238, 260-261; Bach v. County of Butte (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 294, 308.) California Rule of Court 8.278(d)(1) lists the recoverable costs on appeal; no other costs of appeal are recoverable. Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5 lists costs recoverable after trial, and has no bearing on a Memorandum of Costs on Appeal. "The very language and context of section 1033.5 indicates that it does not govern costs on appeal." (Alan S. v. Sup. Ct. (Mary T.), supra, 172 Cal.App.4th 238, 259; see also Stratton v. Beck (2018) 30 CA5th 901, 910.) Ms. Jones challenges four items in the Memorandum of Costs, for a total of $657.50: (1) $43 in additional costs filing the Responsive Brief, above the statutory filing fee of $390; (2) $527 for preparation of the Clerk's Transcript or Appendix, because Ms. Jones argues she paid $1,286 in costs related to preparing the record on appeal and courier fees are not included in that type of cost; (3) $73 in printing and copying costs, because all filings were electronic; (4) $14.50 of the claimed cost for electronic filing, above the statutory filing fee of $10.50. The Motion simultaneously alleges that Ms. Sacco did not actually incur these costs and that these are not authorized costs under CCP section 1033.5(a) and (c)(1). As discussed above, allowable trial costs under CCP section 1033.5 are irrelevant; only California Rule of Court 8.278 governs recoverable costs on appeal. In the Opposition, Ms. Sacco demonstrates that the items challenged in the motion were actually incurred, and the requested costs are recoverable under California Rule of Court 8.278 (See, specifically, Cal. Rules of Court, rules 8.278(d)(1)(A), 8.278(d)(1)(B), 8.278(d)(1)(D), and 8.278(d)(1)(E).) [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Still no courtesy copies of the objections filed on 6/4/21 and 6/7/21 have been received by the Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 B. and C.] No proof of service of the correct Notice of Hearing has been filed for the First Amended Petition filed on 2/5/21. The only proper form of notice for an 850 petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If any party was entitled to be personally served, pursuant to Probate Code Section 851(a), proof of personal service must be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed just before, on, or after the 6/9/21 hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/20/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LESLIE MICHAEL SILVERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why a petition for termination pursuant to Probate Code 12251 hasn't been filed. Attorney filed a request for dismissal which is not proper procedure. LRSF 14.37 Terminating an Estate for Lack of Assets. If a personal representative files a petition for the termination of further proceedings and for discharge pursuant to Probate Code 12251, the petition must list creditors' claims and disclose any action taken on such claims and must address the notice required by Probate Code 9201-9202. Notice must be given to any creditor whose claim has not been satisfied.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE LUIS REYES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/23/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. report of administrator is allowed and the reported acts and transactions of the administrator during the period of this report are allowed. Unreimbursed costs to Hathaway, Perrett, Webster, Powers, Chrisman & Gutierrez is the amount of $780.38 is allowed. Costs for the filing fee for the final petition in the amount of $435.00 is allowed. statutory fees of $24,410.06 to attorney and administrator are approved. reserve of $4,000.00.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIO V. MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/23/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] The address of an interested party cannot be in care of his/her attorney unless that person is an adult and has directed in writing that notice be given to his/her attorney. Although Eloy Martinez's petition was filed before LRSF 14.18 E. became effective on 1/1/2021, a Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) should now be filed for the proposed personal representative Leo Bautista. There were several "Declarations" filed on 4/15/21 objecting to Eloy Martinez's petition. No filing fee was paid; such objections will not be considered until the filing fee for an objection is paid. Courtesy copies of the petition, all supporting and subsequent filings, and any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/13/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Whether Probate Code 6124 applies to the Lost Will. 2) Issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. 3) Issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARY A. PRONGOS 1994 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Peter J Prongos And Catherine M. Giouzelis As General Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE MARGARET STEINKELLNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/2021. Continued again same issues. 1) In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)] The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. 2) Must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/23/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JESSICA G. MCLINTOCK IRREVOCABLE TRUST OF 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions For Order Confirming Trust Interpretation And Directing The Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Although paragraph B.2.(a)(1) of the trust begins with the provision that the trustee shall pay to Scott Alan McClintock all of the net income "during his lifetime or the duration of the trust" and it appears reasonable that the ambiguous language should be interpreted to terminate the trust as or when the other conditions have been met, it is unclear if Scott has "legitimate issue" whose interests could be affected if this trust were to terminate upon final determination of estate taxes and be distributed to him outright. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Although the proposed order was just received today, contrary to LRSF 14.7 A., and no courtesy copy of the proof of service has been provided, contrary to LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C., this Examiner opted to leave this matter on for an appearance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE MARGARET TILLERY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/4/21. the confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form is not yet on file (information must be provided for both petitioners). please note, this attachment must have a caption page and cannot be efiled. deadline for getting the attachments on file is noon on 7/30/21. in addiiton, although the address on the petition and notice of hearing list a san francisco address for both petitioners, according to the request for family allowance, one of the petitioners is currently residing outside the U.S., and would not be eligible for appointment as a personal representative of an estate under probate code section 8402. note, that code section does not refer to domicile, but to residence. no later than noon on 7/30/21, please also file a supplement to the petition addressing this issue. examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.
